rationale: The cited paper proposes a multiple ideal point model with probabilistic switching/Markov transitions among latent ideal points, which is conceptually close to the citing paper’s core contribution of modeling simultaneous latent goals with context-driven weight adaptation in choice. Given this strong methodological overlap (both are multi-latent-preference choice frameworks), the citation could be theory/method foundation rather than incidental background. Even though the raw evidence is only a DOI match, this pair is not safely droppable as mention-only.
